title: CLAUDE.md — GAO / Republican Revolution
text: Title: Republican Revolution Hamstring Congressional Oversight? Evidence 55,000 GAO Reports Author: Jack Rametta (solo) Status: Revising (back review) Presented: MPSA 2024 R package (gao) automated harvesting processing Government Accountability Office reports, plus accompanying research paper analyzing congressional oversight patterns.

what: Harvest United States Government Accountability Office Reports
title: Harvest United States Government Accountability Office Reports
text: gao provides tools downloading reports published United States Government Accountability Office (GAO). ships bundled dataset 55,000 report URLs (1921–present, updated daily via GitHub Actions) one-step function batch downloading reports PDF HTML. Disclaimer: package affiliated , endorsed , way officially connected U.S. Government Accountability Office. data obtained public web pages gao.gov.

title: Harvest United States Government Accountability Office Reports
text: gao requires curl-impersonate system dependency (GAO.gov uses TLS fingerprint filtering blocks standard HTTP clients). install package:
code: # Arch Linux sudo pacman -S curl-impersonate  # macOS brew install lexiforest/curl-impersonate/curl-impersonate # From CRAN (when available) install.packages("gao")  # Development version from GitHub # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("CetiAlphaFive/gao")

title: Harvest United States Government Accountability Office Reports
text: auto_download() loads bundled dataset, filters fiscal year, downloads PDFs, HTMLs, gao_reports/pdf/ gao_reports/html/. ’s .
code: library(gao)  # Interactive --- prompts for format and year range auto_download()  # Or specify everything up front auto_download(format = "pdf", year = 2020:2024, confirm = FALSE)

what: Download GAO Reports in One Step — auto_download
title: Download GAO Reports in One Step — auto_download
text: Convenience wrapper loads bundled report links, optionally filters fiscal year, downloads reports PDF, HTML, . interactive sessions, prompts format year range supplied.

title: Download GAO Reports in One Step — auto_download
code: auto_download(   format = NULL,   year = NULL,   download_dir = "gao_reports",   sleep_time = 1,   confirm = TRUE )

title: Download GAO Reports in One Step — auto_download
text: format Character. "pdf", "html", "". NULL (default) prompts interactively; non-interactive sessions defaults "pdf". year Integer vector 4-digit fiscal years, e.g. 2024 2020:2024. NULL (default) prompts interactively; non-interactive sessions uses available years. download_dir Character. Base directory downloads. pdf/ /html/ subdirectories created beneath . sleep_time Numeric. Seconds pause downloads. confirm Logical. TRUE (default), prompts confirmation downloading. non-interactive sessions, confirm = TRUE raises error prevent accidental mass downloads — set confirm = FALSE explicitly.

title: Download GAO Reports in One Step — auto_download
text: Invisible character vector downloaded file paths.

title: Download GAO Reports in One Step — auto_download
text: PDF URLs constructed directly report IDs (e.g., /products/gao-24-106198 becomes /assets/gao-24-106198.pdf) rather scraping report page, extra HTTP requests needed link extraction.

title: Download GAO Reports in One Step — auto_download
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Interactive: walks through prompts auto_download()  # Non-interactive: download 2024 PDFs auto_download(format = "pdf", year = 2024, confirm = FALSE) } # }

title: Extract GAO Report Links — extract_links
code: extract_links(   base_url = "https://www.gao.gov/reports-testimonies",   last_page = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   save_to_file = FALSE,   sleep_time = 1,   output_file = "gao_report_links.rds" )

title: Extract GAO Report Links — extract_links
text: base_url Character. base URL GAO reports (default: "https://www.gao.gov/reports-testimonies"). last_page Integer. Last page number scrape. NULL, detected automatically pagination. verbose Logical. TRUE, shows progress bar (default: TRUE). save_to_file Logical. TRUE, saves data RDS file (default: FALSE). sleep_time Numeric. Seconds pause page requests. output_file Character. File path output.

title: Extract GAO Report Links — extract_links
text: data.frame columns: url, title, report_id, published, released, summary.

title: Update GAO Report Links — update_links
text: Scrapes recent GAO report listing pages appends new links already bundled dataset. Stops automatically reaches reports already known (3 consecutive pages new links) 3 consecutive fetch failures.

title: Update GAO Report Links — update_links
Update GAO Report Links — update_links
text: verbose Logical. Show progress messages (default: TRUE). sleep_time Numeric. Seconds requests (default: 1).

title: Update GAO Report Links — update_links
text: data.frame known reports (old + new), sorted url.

title: Update GAO Report Links — update_links
gao 0.3.0
text: Breaking: gao_links() now returns data.frame columns url, title, report_id, published, released, summary instead character vector. Bundled dataset switched text (.txt) RDS (.rds) compression rich metadata. Year filtering auto_download() now uses published date fiscal year calculation instead regex report IDs, fixing ~29% reports legacy ID formats previously yielded NA years. extract_links() update_links() now return data.frames full report metadata. Fixed R-CMD-check GitHub Action syntax error (args parameter).

title: gao 0.2.0
text: Added auto_download() convenience wrapper handles full pipeline (load links, filter year, download PDF/HTML) one call. PDF URLs now constructed directly report IDs, avoiding one HTTP request per report compared extract_pdf_links(). Interactive prompts format year range arguments omitted. Non-interactive safety: confirm = TRUE errors unless explicitly set FALSE, preventing accidental mass downloads.

title: gao 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Bundled dataset ~55,000 GAO report URLs (1921–present). gao_links() access bundled report URLs. update_links() scrape newly published reports. extract_links() build full link list scratch. extract_pdf_links() find PDF download links report pages. download_pdfs() download_htmls() batch downloading. Requires ‘curl-impersonate’ TLS fingerprint compatibility.
